THOUGHT:
The reference list currently only contains Sculley et al. (2015), which establishes the high-level motivation regarding ML system debt. To systematically cover the 8 required citation categories across the rounds, the next most critical addition must address the core technical pitfall of **distribution shift** and the **dataset/evaluation gap** between academic benchmarks and production environments. I will target the WILDS benchmark paper. This citation will simultaneously serve multiple purposes: crediting dataset creators (Category 3), highlighting the research gap in i.i.d. evaluation assumptions (Category 5), providing a foundation for comparing robustness and domain adaptation methods (Category 4 & 6), and supporting arguments about why standard training fails in deployment (Category 7). Subsequent rounds will target optimizer instability, specific architectural concepts, and future research directions.
